free_text: Sections of Elemental Tungsten at Energies between
Germany. Main reference, data are partly given
Deuterium gas target, 1.0 cm in diameter and
3.0 cm in length, gas pressure 0.19-0.2 MPa.
.Mean neutron energy is defined within +/- 20 keV.
N-gamma discrimination with pulse shape analysis.
Five NE213 neutron detectors D1-D5 located
behind a massive collimator shielding at a
distance of 1200 cm from the sample. The separation
angle between the detectors is 12.5 deg. Detector
length 2.54 cm (D1)/5.08 cm (D2-D5). The angle region
covered is from 12.5 deg to 160 deg.
.An additional NE213 detector was used as relative
fluence monitor, diameter 3.81 cm, length 3.81 cm.
The movable monitor (in a fixed distance of 651 cm)
was always in a position of 58.9 deg relative
.All cross sections were extracted as elemental ones.
The elastic scattering could not be separated from
inelastic scattering on low-lying levels of all
depends in shape and amplitude on the sum of all
inelastic cross sections applied, the angular
distributions of these inelastic sum cross sections
are also given in a separate section (mean excitation
diameter 25.0 mm, height 49.7 mm, mass 465.52 g,
placed 17.5 cm from the gas target centre.
.Monte Carlo simulated TOF spectra of the complete
angular distribution were iteratively adjusted to
the measured TOF spectra. The simulation comprised
the neutron production in the gas target and the
scattering geometry between target, sample and
.Parasitic neutrons from the D(d,np) breakup reaction
were simulated and subtracted. The data for the
simulation were adjusted by additional measurements
of the neutron scattering with a 4-He(d,np) neutron
Error of the central point of neutron energy
contain an additional uncertainty component to
consider the influence of the chosen maximum order
l-max of the fitted Legendre polynomial on the
Energy error given in digitized form - SM
REACTION and ANALYSIS codes are corrected.
SD: in Subent 026 REACTION code corrected.
*By the authors* "Analysis EL" for smooth cross section
*By the authors*: "Analysis SUM" for cross section
*Emission DDX data are taken into account, SAN 007-014
.Elastic cross sections were obtained by including
nine low-lying levels. The inelastic cross sections
of these nine levels were taken from the ENDF/B-VI
evaluation. The inelastic data (sum of nine inelastic
angular distributions) are given below, SAN 005
.Data for inelastic scattering taken from ENDF/B-VI
